title: Define a group-company relationship
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to define a group-company relationship that links multiple related companies (subsidiaries, sister concerns, or affiliated entities), so that related legal entities can be represented and managed together in the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am authorized to manage the portfolio, when I create a group-company construct, then I can add multiple related companies to it as members.; Given a group-company relationship exists, when I view it, then all linked companies are listed as members of the group.; Given companies are not explicitly added to a group-company construct, when the group is evaluated, then those companies are not treated as related.
source: FR 6.3.1 (definition of group-company relationships with multiple related companies)

title: Enable shared administration across related companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to enable shared administrative scenarios across the related companies in a group, so that administration can be handled centrally for affiliated entities.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group-company construct links related companies, when shared administration is configured, then an authorized admin can perform administrative actions across the linked companies.; Given shared administration is not explicitly authorized, when an admin attempts a cross-company administrative action, then the action is denied.

title: Share locations across related companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to share locations across related companies within the group, so that affiliated entities can reuse common physical locations without duplicating them.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies are linked via the group-company construct, when location sharing is enabled, then a shared location is available to the authorized related companies.; Given location sharing is not explicitly authorized, when a company attempts to use another company's location, then access is not granted.

title: Require explicit authorization for shared scenarios
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want shared administration and shared locations to require explicit authorization, so that cross-company sharing never happens implicitly or by default.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group-company construct exists, when no explicit authorization has been granted, then shared administration and shared location capabilities remain disabled.; Given explicit authorization is granted, when the scenario is used, then only the authorized companies and admins can act across the group.

title: Maintain auditability of shared scenarios
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want all shared administrative and shared-location activity to be auditable, so that cross-company actions can be traced and reviewed for accountability.
acceptance_criteria: Given shared administration or shared locations are used across the group, when an action occurs, then it is recorded in an audit trail.; Given an auditor reviews cross-company activity, when they inspect the audit trail, then they can identify who performed the action, on which company, and when.

title: Enable consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search across linked companies, so that I can view unified information spanning the related entities.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies are explicitly linked via the group-company construct, cross-company access is explicitly enabled, and I hold an explicit group-level role, when I run consolidated reporting or a directory search, then results span the linked companies.; Given any of the three conditions (explicit link, enabled cross-company access, granted group-level role) is not met, when I attempt consolidated reporting or cross-company directory search, then access is denied.
source: FR 6.3.3 (consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search prerequisites)

title: Grant explicit group-level roles for cross-company access
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to grant explicit group-level roles to users, so that only appropriately privileged users can access cross-company reporting and directory features.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user has not been granted a group-level role, when they attempt cross-company access, then the request is rejected.; Given a user is granted an explicit group-level role and cross-company access is enabled, when they access group features, then they can perform consolidated reporting and cross-company directory search.

title: Enforce security constraints on cross-company access
user_story: As a System, I want to enforce row-level security filters and maintain an audit trail for all cross-company access, so that group-company data access remains scoped and traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user performs cross-company reporting or directory search, when results are returned, then row-level security filters restrict data to what the user is authorized to see.; Given any cross-company access occurs, when the access is processed, then an audit trail entry is recorded for it.
source: FR 6.3.3 (row-level security filters; audit trail for all cross-company access)
